meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Scale Your Freelance Workflow Efficiently
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with scaling. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-end apps faster than your competition.
content.answer_block: To scale your freelance business, you must move beyond building from scratch. Implementing a robust SaaS starter kit allows you to standardize authentication, billing, and database schemas. By leveraging AI-driven CLI tools like Claude Code and Antigravity, you can automate repetitive boilerplate, slashing delivery times and increasing your profit margins.
content.intro:
The ceiling of freelance development is brutal: you are trading hours for dollars, and every new client project feels like a grueling ascent from zero. You spend your first week configuring Auth0, Stripe webhooks, and database migrations, leaving you exhausted before a single line of business logic is written. This isn't just inefficient; it's a bottleneck that kills your ability to scale.

But the paradigm has shifted. Top-tier builders no longer code from a blank editor; they orchestrate high-velocity systems. By integrating a modular SaaS starter kit with intelligent CLI agents, you transform your workflow from manual labor into a scalable, repeatable assembly line. It is time to stop building components and start building empires.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project using a battle-tested template that includes pre-configured database ORM and authentication flows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean SaaS boilerplate using Next.js 15, Drizzle ORM, and Auth.js. Include a modular directory structure for API routes and server actions.

steps.title: Configure Stripe Webhooks
steps.description: Automate payment processing to ensure you aren't manually managing subscription states for every client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust Stripe webhook handler that updates user subscription status in the database based on 'invoice.payment_succeeded' events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts --task 'implement event validation and db sync'

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Synchronize your local environment with production schemas using AI-assisted migration scripts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Drizzle migration script for a 'projects' table with tenant-based row-level security and foreign keys to the 'users' table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate migration for projects schema and apply to local db'

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
steps.description: Secure your SaaS by defining granular permissions that are enforced at the middleware level.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an RBAC middleware that checks user roles from the session and redirects unauthorized access to the landing page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file middleware.ts --task 'add rbac logic for admin and user roles'

steps.title: Deploy via CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Ensure your production environment is automatically updated whenever you push code, reducing manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and deploys the app to Vercel upon merging to main.

pro_tips: Always wrap your database queries in a repository pattern so you can swap ORMs or databases without rewriting business logic.
Use environment-specific secret management; never hardcode API keys, even in a starter kit environment.
Implement 'Feature Flags' early, allowing you to deploy code to production that remains hidden from users until the client approves.
Standardize your logging across the entire stack using a service like Axiom or Sentry to debug production issues in seconds, not hours.
Keep your UI components in a separate package or library folder to ensure your design system remains consistent across multiple client projects.
content.next_steps: After establishing your base, focus on creating a documentation site for your clients using your new boilerplate. This adds immense value and ensures they can manage their own SaaS after you hand off the keys.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to stop struggling with project scaling and start dominating the freelance market. Stop manually building the same infrastructure for every client. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
